scope.summary: Implement a model-steerable avatar pose generation tool that accepts a natural-language pose description or a reference image ArtifactStore ref, invokes the kernel LlmClient with a pose-estimation prompt, and converts the output joint rotations to a PoseEditRequest sequence that drives the IK-mode avatar joint updater
acceptance_criteria: A generate_avatar_pose MCP tool is registered in TailorModelAdapter accepting pose_description: String and optional reference_image_artifact_id: String
The tool builds a ContextBundle with the current AVT- measurements + joint list and dispatches via ModelAdapter to the configured LlmClient
Model output is a JSON array of PoseEditRequest objects (joint_name, target_rotation_deg: [f32;3]); it is validated against the canonical joint name set before any proxy update
The validated pose is applied through the IK solver (from the IK Mode MT) and TailorBodyProxyUpdated is emitted
The tool works with any LlmClient implementation (local/CLI/BYOK) registered in the kernel; no cloud-specific provider is required
cargo test avatar_pose_gen::test_pose_generation_via_mock_llm passes: mock LLM returns 6-joint pose, IK drives the proxy, event is emitted
scope.risk_if_missed: MD's AI Pose Generator is the highest-visibility AI feature in 2025.1. Without a kernel-native equivalent, Tailor's model-first differentiator applies to garment authoring but not avatar setup, leaving a prominent gap when compared to MD. The IK infrastructure (proposed above) makes this MT cheap to add.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
